Code §§ 47-22-4, 47-22-7), a new-consideration recital for covenants signed after employment begins, customer restrictions drafted as true non-piracy provisions, employee non-solicitation, an optional non-compete tied to identified protectable interests and sized to the actual footprint, a sale-of-business acknowledgement under the lesser-scrutiny standard, and physician covenant limits capped at one year and thirty road miles with voidness on employer termination (W. Va. Code § 47-11E-1 to -5). Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Restrictive Covenant Wisconsin](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-restrictive-covenant-wisconsin): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Wisconsin-specific employee restrictive covenant agreement built for Wis. Stat. § 103.465, where a covenant is enforceable only if reasonably necessary to protect the employer, a five-factor reasonableness test governs every post-employment restraint, and an unreasonable restraint is void in full with no judicial blue-penciling. Confidentiality and trade-secret protection scoped to protectable information, customer and employee non-solicitation (the employee no-poach limited to actual working relationships), an optional non-compete drawn to the five-factor reasonableness standard, a consideration recital resting on genuine forbearance, no extension-during-breach tolling that would void the restraint, Wisconsin governing law rather than a foreign choice-of-law clause, and divisible covenants in place of reliance on reformation. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Restrictive Covenant Wyoming](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-restrictive-covenant-wyoming): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Wyoming-specific employee restrictive covenant agreement with modular covenant structure (non-compete, non-solicitation, non-dealing, non-investment, confidentiality, non-disparagement). Incorporates Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108 (SF 107, 2025) pathway gating for non-compete-adjacent covenants. Drafted as a starting point for review by licensed counsel.

### Professional Services

- [Professional Services Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/bonterms-cc0-1.0/bonterms-professional-services-agreement): Bonterms · CC0-1.0 — Cover page for the Bonterms Professional Services Agreement (Version 1.2). The standard terms are incorporated by reference from bonterms.com.
- [Independent Contractor Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-independent-contractor-agreement): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — An independent contractor agreement based on Common Paper's standard form. Covers the engagement of an independent contractor, including scope of services, rates, payment, timeline, and governing law.
- [Professional Services Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-professional-services-agreement): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — A professional services agreement with key terms, statement of work, and standard terms, based on Common Paper's standard form. Covers consulting and professional services engagements including deliverables, IP ownership, fees, and liability.
- [Statement Of Work](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-statement-of-work): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — A statement of work template for professional services engagements, based on Common Paper's standard form. References a PSA or CSA Key Terms and covers scope, deliverables, timeline, fees, and expenses.

### Data & Compliance

- [AI Addendum](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-ai-addendum): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — An AI addendum cover page and standard terms, based on Common Paper's standard form. Adds AI-specific provisions to an existing agreement, covering model training, input/output rights, and AI usage policies.
- [AI Addendum In App](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-ai-addendum-in-app): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — An in-app AI addendum based on Common Paper's standard form. A streamlined version of the AI addendum designed to be accepted electronically within an application.
- [Business Associate Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-business-associate-agreement): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — A HIPAA business associate agreement cover page and standard terms, based on Common Paper's standard form. Covers the use and protection of protected health information (PHI) between a covered entity and a business associate.
- [Data Processing Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/common-paper-cc-by-4.0/common-paper-data-processing-agreement): Common Paper · CC-BY-4.0 — A data processing agreement cover page and standard terms, based on Common Paper's standard form. Covers GDPR and data protection compliance, including processor/controller roles, data transfers, subprocessors, and security measures.

### Deal Process

- [Closing Checklist](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc0-1.0/openagreements-closing-checklist): OpenAgreements · CC0-1.0 — A stage-first, document-first closing checklist that tracks canonical documents, action items, and open issues for a transaction, and renders a formatted document for distribution to the working group.
- [Due Diligence Request List](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-due-diligence-request-list): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Modular legal due diligence request list (DDRL) for corporate transactions. Ships a base form covering organization, capitalization, contracts, IP, employment, litigation, compliance, tax, insurance, debt, property, and privacy/cybersecurity. Four optional industry riders gate via boolean toggles: tech / SaaS, life sciences, healthcare provider, and cross-border / trade compliance. Drafted clean-room from public-source consensus across 15 Am Law firm, bar association, and legal publisher DDRLs; no public source language is reused.
- [Working Group List](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc0-1.0/openagreements-working-group-list): OpenAgreements · CC0-1.0 — Standalone working group roster for a transaction. Tracks staffed deal team members and their contact details outside the closing checklist document.

### Other

- [Board Ratifying Consent](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-board-ratifying-consent): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Action by unanimous written consent of the board of directors of a Delaware corporation ratifying one or more defective corporate acts under Section 204 of the Delaware General Corporation Law — reciting each act, its date, any putative stock, and the nature of the failure of authorization, and directing the stockholder submission, certificate of validation, and notice the statute requires.
- [Confidentiality Invention Assignment Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-confidentiality-invention-assignment-agreement): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Employee proprietary information and inventions assignment agreement with explicit prior-inventions and personal-project carveout language placeholders.
- [Confidentiality Invention Assignment Agreement Nevada](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-confidentiality-invention-assignment-agreement-nevada): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Nevada-specific employee confidentiality and invention assignment agreement aligned with Nevada's employer-ownership default (NRS 600.500): the employer keeps statutory ownership of patentable inventions and trade secrets developed in the course and scope of employment that relate directly to the work, the own-time carve-out reaches only inventions outside that statutory grant, and the form carries no state carve-out notice because Nevada imposes none. The assignment clause keeps present-tense assignment language for a clean federal patent chain of title and carries no post-employment holdover tail. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder IP Confidentiality Confirmation](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-ip-confidentiality-confirmation): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — A short confirmatory certificate signed by a departing founder and countersigned by the Delaware corporation confirming that the founder's existing Confidential Information and Invention Assignment Agreement (CIIAA/PIIA) and confidentiality obligations remain in full force after service ends, that all company property and repositories have been returned, and that no release is intended as to those continuing obligations. It is confirmatory only and creates no new restrictive covenant. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder Separation Cap Table Update Instructions](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-separation-cap-table-update-instructions): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Written instructions from an authorized officer of a Delaware corporation to the corporate secretary, transfer agent, or cap-table administrator to record a completed founder share repurchase: cancel or take possession of the repurchased certificates (or make the book-entry transfer for uncertificated shares), update the stock ledger, and reflect the disposition — cancelled and retired or held as treasury — on the cap table. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder Separation Records Checklist](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-separation-records-checklist): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — A closing certificate and filing checklist for assembling a founder departure's executed instruments into a Delaware corporation's minute book: the board written consent, resignation letter, repurchase notice and repurchase agreement, proof of payment, any stockholder-consent notices, the IP/confidentiality confirmation, and the stock-ledger and cap-table updates. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder Separation Resignation Letter](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-separation-resignation-letter): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — A short letter from a departing founder to the board of directors of a Delaware corporation resigning from every officer and director position held, with an explicit effective date. The effective date is load-bearing: it fixes the termination date that starts the repurchase-option window under the founder's existing Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder Share Repurchase Notice](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-share-repurchase-notice): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Written notice from a Delaware corporation to a departing founder electing to exercise the company's pre-existing right to repurchase the founder's unvested shares within the exercise window set by the founder's Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement, identifying the governing agreement, termination date, unvested share count, per-share and aggregate price, and closing date. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder Stock Assignment Separate](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-stock-assignment-separate): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — A stock power (assignment separate from the certificate) by which a founder, for value received, assigns repurchased shares of a Delaware corporation back to the corporation and appoints an attorney-in-fact to transfer the shares on the corporation's books. Often pre-signed at issuance and held with the Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement or in escrow; here it effects the transfer of the repurchased shares. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Founder Stock Repurchase Agreement](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-founder-stock-repurchase-agreement): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Agreement between a Delaware corporation and a departing founder consummating the repurchase and cancellation of the founder's shares, whether as a negotiated buyback or as a belt-and-suspenders confirmation of a repurchase right exercised under the founder's existing Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement, with an optional mutual-release rider. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
- [Stockholder Ratifying Consent](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/tree/main/templates/openagreements-cc-by-4.0/openagreements-stockholder-ratifying-consent): OpenAgreements · CC-BY-4.0 — Action by written consent of the stockholders of a Delaware corporation approving the board's ratification of a defective corporate act under Section 204 of the Delaware General Corporation Law — approving the specifically identified act, and approving any related charter amendment or certificate of validation the cure requires.

## Optional

- [Using OpenAgreements with AI agents & MCP](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/blob/main/docs/supported-tools.md): local + hosted MCP servers, agent skills, and the CLI for programmatic template filling
- [openagreements.org](https://openagreements.org): machine-readable JSON/CSV twins of every guide, survey, checklist, and template
- [Contributing](https://github.com/open-agreements/open-agreements/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): propose a form source, request coverage, or open a pull request
